---
title: llms-full.txt
description: Complete documentation for ShadSheet in a single file for AI tools and LLM indexing.
---

# ShadSheet Documentation

> Headless React spreadsheet component library built with TanStack Table, HyperFormula, and shadcn conventions.

## Table of Contents
- [Introduction](#introduction)
- [Installation](#installation)
- [Quick Start](#quick-start)
- [Concepts](#concepts)
- [Components](#components)
- [Hooks](#hooks)
- [Utilities](#utilities)
- [Guides](#guides)

---

## Introduction

ShadSheet is a headless React spreadsheet component library that gives you full control over styling while handling all the complex logic.

### Why ShadSheet?

#### Headless-First

Unlike traditional spreadsheet components that lock you into specific styling, ShadSheet exports:

- **Hooks** for state management (`useSpreadsheetStore`, `useCellSelection`, `useClipboard`)
- **Sub-components** for composition (`Toolbar`, `FormulaBar`, `SpreadsheetGrid`)
- **Utilities** for data operations (`exportToCSV`, `validateCellValue`)

Build your own UI or use the batteries-included `Spreadsheet` component.

#### Layered Architecture

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│  <Spreadsheet />  (batteries-included)  │  ← Default: import and go
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Sub-components: Toolbar, FormulaBar,   │  ← Compose your own layout
│  Grid, StatusBar, ColumnHeaders, etc.   │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Hooks: useSpreadsheetStore, useCell,   │  ← Full control over state
│  useSelection, useClipboard, etc.       │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Utils: export, filter, formula, merge  │  ← Standalone utilities
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│  Types: all public interfaces           │  ← TypeScript-first
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

#### Feature-Rich

- **Virtualization** - Handle 100K+ rows with TanStack Virtual
- **Formulas** - Full Excel-compatible formulas via HyperFormula
- **Selection** - Range selection, keyboard navigation, clipboard
- **Filtering** - Global search + per-column expression filters
- **Sorting** - Multi-column sort with TanStack Table
- **Pinning** - Left and right pinned columns
- **Merging** - Cell merging with undo/redo
- **Export** - CSV and XLSX download
- **Formatting** - Bold, italic, colors, alignment
- **Theming** - Light/dark mode, Tailwind CSS

### Quick Comparison

| Feature | ShadSheet | TanStack Table | React Data Grid |
|---------|-----------|----------------|-----------------|
| Headless | ✓ (layered) | ✓ | ✗ |
| Formulas | ✓ (HyperFormula) | ✗ | ✓ |
| Virtualization | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Cell Merging | ✓ | ✗ | ✓ |
| Bundle Size | ~150KB | ~45KB | ~200KB |
| Styling | Tailwind/shadcn | BYO | Built-in |

### When to Use ShadSheet

**Good fit:**

- Building data-intensive React applications
- Need spreadsheet-like editing with formulas
- Want headless control over styling
- Using Tailwind CSS and shadcn/ui

**Consider alternatives:**

- Simple tables without editing → TanStack Table
- Need complete built-in styling → AG Grid, React Data Grid
- Non-React environments

---

## Installation

### Requirements

- React 19+
- Node.js 20+
- Tailwind CSS 4+

### Install Package

```bash
npm install @shadsheet/ui
```

#### Peer Dependencies

ShadSheet requires these peer dependencies:

```bash
npm install react react-dom lucide-react @base-ui/react
```

### Import Styles

Import the CSS in your app entry point:

```tsx
// main.tsx or App.tsx
import "@shadsheet/ui/style.css";
```

> Note: The CSS import is **required**. It's not auto-imported to support headless usage.

### Tailwind Configuration

Ensure your `tailwind.config.js` includes ShadSheet's classes:

```js
// tailwind.config.js
export default {
  content: [
    "./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}",
    "./node_modules/@shadsheet/ui/dist/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx}",
  ],
  // ... rest of config
}
```

### Verify Installation

Create a simple spreadsheet to verify everything works:

```tsx
import { Spreadsheet, type ColumnConfig } from "@shadsheet/ui";
import "@shadsheet/ui/style.css";

type Row = {
  name: string;
  amount: number;
  active: boolean;
};

const columns: ColumnConfig<Row>[] = [
  { id: "name", header: "Name", editable: true, width: 180 },
  { id: "amount", header: "Amount", type: "number", editable: true },
  { id: "active", header: "Active", type: "checkbox", editable: true },
];

const data: Row[] = [
  { name: "Alpha", amount: 100, active: true },
  { name: "Beta", amount: 200, active: false },
];

export function App() {
  return (
    <div style={{ height: 400 }}>
      <Spreadsheet data={data} columns={columns} />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Next Steps

- [Quick Start](#quick-start) - Build your first spreadsheet
- [Concepts](#concepts) - Understand the architecture
- [Headless Usage](#headless-usage) - Use hooks and sub-components

---

## Quick Start

### Basic Spreadsheet

The simplest way to use ShadSheet is the `Spreadsheet` component:

```tsx
import { Spreadsheet, type ColumnConfig } from "@shadsheet/ui";
import "@shadsheet/ui/style.css";

type Row = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: string;
};

const columns: ColumnConfig<Row>[] = [
  { id: "id", header: "ID", width: 80 },
  { id: "name", header: "Name", editable: true, width: 200 },
  { id: "email", header: "Email", editable: true, width: 250 },
  { id: "role", header: "Role", type: "select", editable: true, options: [
    { label: "Admin", value: "admin" },
    { label: "User", value: "user" },
    { label: "Guest", value: "guest" },
  ]},
];

const data: Row[] = [
  { id: "1", name: "Alice", email: "alice@example.com", role: "admin" },
  { id: "2", name: "Bob", email: "bob@example.com", role: "user" },
];

export function App() {
  return (
    <div style={{ height: 500 }}>
      <Spreadsheet
        data={data}
        columns={columns}
        sortable
        filterable
        editable
        onCellChange={({ rowIndex, columnId, newValue }) => {
          console.log(`Cell ${rowIndex}.${columnId} changed to:`, newValue);
        }}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

### Column Types

ShadSheet supports several column types:

#### Text

```tsx
{ id: "name", header: "Name", type: "text", editable: true }
```

#### Number

```tsx
{ id: "amount", header: "Amount", type: "number", editable: true }
```

#### Checkbox

```tsx
{ id: "active", header: "Active", type: "checkbox", editable: true }
```

#### Date

```tsx
{ id: "created", header: "Created", type: "date", editable: true }
```

#### Select

```tsx
{
  id: "status",
  header: "Status",
  type: "select",
  editable: true,
  options: [
    { label: "Pending", value: "pending" },
    { label: "Approved", value: "approved" },
    { label: "Rejected", value: "rejected" },
  ]
}
```

### Cell Changes

Handle cell edits with `onCellChange`:

```tsx
<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  onCellChange={({ rowIndex, columnId, oldValue, newValue, rowData }) => {
    // Update your state/backend
    updateRow(rowIndex, columnId, newValue);

    // Return false to veto the change
    // return false;
  }}
/>
```

### Formulas

Enable formulas with the `formulasEnabled` prop:

```tsx
const data = [
  { item: "Widget", price: 100, quantity: 5, total: "=B1*C1" },
  { item: "Gadget", price: 50, quantity: 10, total: "=B2*C2" },
];

<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  formulasEnabled
  showFormulaBar
/>
```

> Note: Formulas use HyperFormula syntax (Excel-compatible).

### Selection Tracking

Track cell selection changes:

```tsx
<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  onSelectionChange={(selection) => {
    console.log("Active cell:", selection.activeCell);
    console.log("Selection range:", selection.selectionRange);
    console.log("Selected rows:", selection.selectedRows);
  }}
/>
```

### Ref API

Use a ref for imperative control:

```tsx
import { useRef } from "react";
import { Spreadsheet, type SpreadsheetRef } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function MyComponent() {
  const ref = useRef<SpreadsheetRef>(null);

  const handleExport = () => {
    ref.current?.exportToCSV();
  };

  const handleScroll = () => {
    ref.current?.scrollToCell(50, "name");
  };

  return (
    <>
      <button onClick={handleExport}>Export CSV</button>
      <Spreadsheet ref={ref} data={data} columns={columns} />
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Ref Methods

| Method | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `focus()` | Focus the grid for keyboard input |
| `scrollToCell(row, col)` | Scroll to a specific cell |
| `getSelectedData()` | Get selected range as 2D array |
| `getData()` | Get all row data |
| `setData(data)` | Replace all data |
| `exportToCSV()` | Download as CSV |
| `exportToXLSX()` | Download as XLSX |
| `undo()` | Undo last change |
| `redo()` | Redo last undone change |

---

## Concepts

### Provider Pattern

ShadSheet uses React contexts to share state across components. The `SpreadsheetProvider` wraps your spreadsheet UI and provides:

- **SpreadsheetContext** - Zustand store for UI state
- **TableContext** - TanStack Table instance
- **HyperFormulaContext** - Formula engine instance

```tsx
import {
  SpreadsheetProvider,
  SpreadsheetGrid,
  Toolbar,
  FormulaBar,
} from "@shadsheet/ui";

function CustomSpreadsheet({ data, columns }) {
  return (
    <SpreadsheetProvider data={data} columns={columns}>
      <div className="flex flex-col h-full">
        <Toolbar columns={columns} />
        <FormulaBar />
        <SpreadsheetGrid className="flex-1" />
      </div>
    </SpreadsheetProvider>
  );
}
```

> Warning: Hooks like `useSpreadsheetStore` and `useHyperFormula` must be used within a `SpreadsheetProvider`.

### State Management

ShadSheet uses Zustand with Immer for state management. The store holds:

- **Selection state** - Active cell, selection range
- **Edit state** - Currently editing cell, value, validation errors
- **Table state** - Sorting, filters, column order, pinning
- **UI state** - Cell formats, merged cells, context menu

```tsx
import { useSpreadsheetStore } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function SelectionIndicator() {
  const activeCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.activeCell);
  const selectionRange = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.selectionRange);

  if (!activeCell) return <span>No selection</span>;

  return (
    <span>
      {selectionRange
        ? `Range: ${selectionRange.start.rowIndex}:${selectionRange.end.rowIndex}`
        : `Cell: ${activeCell.rowIndex}, ${activeCell.columnId}`}
    </span>
  );
}
```

### Data Flow

```
┌─────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐     ┌─────────────┐
│   Props     │────▶│ SpreadsheetProvider │────▶│ TanStack Table │
│ data, cols  │     │   + HyperFormula  │     │   + Zustand   │
└─────────────┘     └──────────────────┘     └─────────────┘
                                                   │
                    ┌──────────────────────────────┘
                    ▼
              ┌─────────────┐
              │ Components  │
              │ Grid, Toolbar│
              └─────────────┘
                    │
                    ▼
              ┌─────────────┐
              │   Callbacks │
              │ onCellChange│
              └─────────────┘
```

#### HyperFormula Integration

Cell values are stored in HyperFormula, not React state. This enables:

- Excel-compatible formulas
- Undo/redo history
- Dependency tracking
- Cross-sheet references (future)

```tsx
import { useHyperFormula } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function FormulaViewer() {
  const hf = useHyperFormula();

  if (!hf) return null;

  // Access raw formula
  const formula = hf.getCellFormula({ sheet: 0, row: 0, col: 0 });

  // Access computed value
  const value = hf.getCellValue({ sheet: 0, row: 0, col: 0 });

  return <div>{formula ?? String(value)}</div>;
}
```

### Column Configuration

Columns define the schema and behavior:

```tsx
interface ColumnConfig<TData> {
  id: string;                    // Column identifier (maps to data key)
  header: string;                // Display name
  width?: number;                // Initial width in pixels
  minWidth?: number;             // Minimum resizable width
  maxWidth?: number;             // Maximum resizable width
  pinned?: "left" | "right";     // Pin to edge
  sortable?: boolean;            // Enable sorting
  filterable?: boolean;          // Enable filtering
  type?: "text" | "number" | "date" | "select" | "checkbox";
  editable?: boolean | ((row: TData) => boolean);  // Edit permission
  validation?: ZodSchema;        // Zod validation schema
  options?: SelectOption[];      // Options for select type
}
```

### Selection Model

ShadSheet supports multiple selection modes:

#### Cell Selection

```tsx
const activeCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.activeCell);
// { rowIndex: 0, columnId: "name" }
```

#### Range Selection

```tsx
const selectionRange = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.selectionRange);
// { start: { rowIndex: 0, columnId: "a" }, end: { rowIndex: 5, columnId: "c" } }
```

#### Row Selection

```tsx
<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  rowSelection="multi"  // "single" | "multi" | "none"
  onSelectionChange={(selection) => {
    console.log(selection.selectedRowIds);  // ["row-1", "row-3"]
    console.log(selection.selectedRows);    // [{...}, {...}]
  }}
/>
```

### Virtualization

ShadSheet uses TanStack Virtual for both rows and columns:

- **Rows** - Virtualized scrolling for 100K+ rows
- **Center columns** - Virtualized horizontal scrolling
- **Pinned columns** - Not virtualized (always visible)

> Note: Virtualization is automatic. No configuration needed.

---

## Components

### Spreadsheet

The `Spreadsheet` component is the primary entry point. It includes all features: toolbar, formula bar, grid, status bar, and context menu.

#### Props

```tsx
import { Spreadsheet, type SpreadsheetProps } from "@shadsheet/ui";
```

##### Data Props

| Prop | Type | Required | Description |
|------|------|----------|-------------|
| `data` | `TData[]` | ✓ | Array of row objects |
| `columns` | `ColumnConfig<TData>[]` | ✓ | Column definitions |
| `getRowId` | `(row, index) => string` | | Custom row ID getter |
| `getSubRows` | `(row) => TData[]` | | Tree data support |

##### Feature Flags

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `sortable` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable column sorting |
| `filterable` | `boolean` | `false` | Enable column filtering |
| `editable` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable cell editing |
| `resizableColumns` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable column resize |
| `formulasEnabled` | `boolean` | `true` | Enable HyperFormula |
| `showToolbar` | `boolean` | `true` | Show toolbar |
| `showFormulaBar` | `boolean` | `true` | Show formula bar |
| `globalSearchable` | `boolean` | `false` | Show global search |

##### Layout Props

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `height` | `string \| number` | `"100%"` | Container height |
| `className` | `string` | | Additional CSS classes |
| `theme` | `"light" \| "dark"` | | Theme override |
| `defaultColumnWidth` | `number` | `120` | Default column width |
| `pinnedColumns` | `{ left?: string[], right?: string[] }` | | Pinned column IDs |
| `grouping` | `string[]` | | Columns to group by |

##### Row Selection

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
|------|------|---------|-------------|
| `rowSelection` | `"single" \| "multi" \| "none"` | `"multi"` | Row selection mode |

##### Callbacks

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `onCellChange` | `(change) => void \| boolean` | Cell edit callback. Return `false` to veto. |
| `onSelectionChange` | `(selection) => void` | Selection changed |
| `onBeforeCellEdit` | `(cell, row) => boolean` | Block edit entry. Return `false` to block. |
| `onSort` | `(sorting) => void` | Sort changed |
| `onFilter` | `(filters) => void` | Filter changed |
| `onExport` | `(format) => void` | Export triggered |

##### Other

| Prop | Type | Description |
|------|------|-------------|
| `exportFileName` | `string` | Default filename for exports |
| `mergeVirtualized` | `boolean` | Optimize merge rendering for large grids |

#### Basic Usage

```tsx
import { Spreadsheet, type ColumnConfig } from "@shadsheet/ui";

type Row = {
  name: string;
  amount: number;
  active: boolean;
};

const columns: ColumnConfig<Row>[] = [
  { id: "name", header: "Name", editable: true, width: 200 },
  { id: "amount", header: "Amount", type: "number", editable: true },
  { id: "active", header: "Active", type: "checkbox", editable: true },
];

const data: Row[] = [
  { name: "Alice", amount: 100, active: true },
  { name: "Bob", amount: 200, active: false },
];

export function MySpreadsheet() {
  return (
    <div style={{ height: 500 }}>
      <Spreadsheet
        data={data}
        columns={columns}
        sortable
        filterable
        onCellChange={({ rowIndex, columnId, newValue }) => {
          console.log(`Changed ${rowIndex}.${columnId} to ${newValue}`);
        }}
      />
    </div>
  );
}
```

#### With Ref

```tsx
import { useRef } from "react";
import { Spreadsheet, type SpreadsheetRef } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function MyComponent() {
  const ref = useRef<SpreadsheetRef>(null);

  return (
    <>
      <button onClick={() => ref.current?.exportToCSV()}>
        Export CSV
      </button>
      <Spreadsheet ref={ref} data={data} columns={columns} />
    </>
  );
}
```

#### Tree Data

```tsx
const data = [
  {
    name: "Parent",
    amount: 0,
    children: [
      { name: "Child 1", amount: 100 },
      { name: "Child 2", amount: 200 },
    ],
  },
];

<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  getSubRows={(row) => row.children}
/>
```

#### Pinned Columns

```tsx
<Spreadsheet
  data={data}
  columns={columns}
  pinnedColumns={{
    left: ["id", "name"],
    right: ["actions"]
  }}
/>
```

### SpreadsheetGrid

Virtualized grid renderer that displays the spreadsheet data. Should be used within a `SpreadsheetProvider`.

### Toolbar

Formatting toolbar component for the spreadsheet. Provides common actions like cut, copy, paste, and formatting options.

### FormulaBar

Input bar for formulas and cell values. Displays the active cell's content and allows formula editing.

### StatusBar

Shows row count, column count, and aggregate information. Also displays the current selection range.

### ColumnHeaders

Header row component that shows column names and handles column-level operations like sorting, filtering, and resizing.

### GlobalSearchFilter

Component for searching and filtering across all columns simultaneously.

### ColumnFilterPanel

Provides column-specific filtering options, allowing for advanced filtering on individual columns.

### ContextMenu

Right-click context menu with common spreadsheet operations like cut, copy, paste, insert/delete rows/columns, etc.

---

## Hooks

### useSpreadsheetStore

Access the internal Zustand store to read and manipulate spreadsheet state.

#### Usage

```tsx
import { useSpreadsheetStore } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function SelectionInfo() {
  const activeCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.activeCell);
  const selectionRange = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.selectionRange);
  const setActiveCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.setActiveCell);

  return (
    <div>
      Active: {activeCell ? `${activeCell.rowIndex}:${activeCell.columnId}` : "None"}
      <button onClick={() => setActiveCell({ rowIndex: 0, columnId: "A" })}>
        Reset
      </button>
    </div>
  );
}
```

> Warning: Must be used within a `SpreadsheetProvider`.

#### State Properties

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `activeCell` | `CellAddress \| null` | Currently focused cell |
| `selectionRange` | `SelectionRange \| null` | Selected range |
| `editingCell` | `CellAddress \| null` | Cell being edited |
| `editValue` | `CellValue` | Current edit value |
| `sorting` | `SortingState` | TanStack sort state |
| `columnFilters` | `ColumnFiltersState` | TanStack filter state |
| `globalFilter` | `string` | Global search value |
| `columnPinning` | `ColumnPinningState` | Pinned columns |
| `columnVisibility` | `VisibilityState` | Hidden columns |
| `rowSelection` | `RowSelectionState` | Selected row IDs |
| `cellFormats` | `Record<string, CellFormat>` | Cell formatting |
| `mergedCells` | `MergedCell[]` | Merged regions |

#### Actions

| Action | Signature | Description |
|--------|-----------|-------------|
| `setActiveCell` | `(cell) => void` | Set focused cell |
| `setSelection` | `(range) => void` | Set selection range |
| `startEditing` | `(cell, overwrite?) => void` | Begin editing |
| `cancelEdit` | `() => void` | Cancel editing |
| `setSorting` | `(sorting) => void` | Update sort state |
| `setGlobalFilter` | `(filter) => void` | Update search |
| `pinColumn` | `(id, position) => void` | Pin/unpin column |
| `toggleBold` | `(keys) => void` | Toggle bold |
| `mergeCells` | `(range, ids) => void` | Merge selection |

### useCellSelection

Manage cell and range selection logic. Provides methods to select ranges, extend selections, and track selection changes.

### useClipboard

Handle copy, cut, and paste operations in the spreadsheet. Integrates with the system clipboard and manages clipboard data format.

### useKeyboardNavigation

Handle keyboard-based navigation in the spreadsheet including arrow keys, Tab, Enter, and other common navigation patterns.

### useHyperFormula

Access the HyperFormula instance directly to perform advanced formula operations, access raw cell formulas, and interact with the formula engine.

### useMergeCells

Manage cell merging operations including creating, removing, and querying merged cell ranges.

### useColumnResize

Handle column resizing operations with proper event handling and UI feedback.

### useGridOperations

Provide access to core grid operations like inserting/deleting rows and columns, clearing cells, and other bulk operations.

### useAutoFill

Handle the auto-fill functionality for copying formulas and values to adjacent cells.

---

## Utilities

### Export Utilities

Standalone functions for exporting spreadsheet data.

#### exportToCSV

Export to CSV file download:

```tsx
import { exportToCSV } from "@shadsheet/ui";
import type { HyperFormula } from "hyperformula";

// Within a component that has hf instance
function handleExport(hf: HyperFormula, columns, columnVisibility) {
  exportToCSV(
    hf,                    // HyperFormula instance
    0,                     // Sheet ID
    columns,               // Column configs
    columnVisibility,      // Visibility state
    "my-data.csv",         // Filename
    { mergedCells, mergedCellLookup },  // Optional merge state
    visibleColumnIds       // Optional column order
  );
}
```

##### Parameters

| Param | Type | Description |
|-------|------|-------------|
| `hf` | `HyperFormula` | Formula engine instance |
| `sheetId` | `number` | Sheet index (usually 0) |
| `columns` | `SpreadsheetColumnConfig[]` | Column definitions |
| `columnVisibility` | `VisibilityState` | Hidden column map |
| `filename` | `string` | Output filename |
| `mergeState` | `MergeExportState` | Merged cell state |
| `orderedVisibleColumnIds` | `string[]` | Custom column order |

#### exportToXLSX

Export to Excel file:

```tsx
import { exportToXLSX } from "@shadsheet/ui";

async function handleExport(hf, columns, columnVisibility) {
  await exportToXLSX(
    hf,
    0,
    columns,
    columnVisibility,
    "my-data.xlsx"
  );
}
```

> Note: XLSX export includes merged cells and number formatting.

#### CSV Injection Protection

The CSV export automatically sanitizes values starting with `=`, `+`, `-`, `@` to prevent formula injection attacks.

### Filter Utilities

Functions for applying and manipulating filters on spreadsheet data.

### Validation Utilities

Zod-based validation utilities for cell values and data types.

### Cell Address Utilities

Functions for parsing and formatting cell addresses in A1 notation.

---

## Guides

### Headless Usage

ShadSheet exports a layered API that lets you build completely custom spreadsheet UIs while reusing the core logic.

#### When to Go Headless

- Need complete control over layout and styling
- Building a domain-specific spreadsheet interface
- Integrating with existing UI components
- Performance optimization (render only what you need)

#### Basic Custom Layout

```tsx
import {
  SpreadsheetProvider,
  SpreadsheetGrid,
  Toolbar,
  FormulaBar,
  StatusBar,
  useSpreadsheetStore,
} from "@shadsheet/ui";
import "@shadsheet/ui/style.css";

function CustomSpreadsheet({ data, columns }) {
  const activeCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.activeCell);

  return (
    <SpreadsheetProvider data={data} columns={columns}>
      <div className="flex flex-col h-full border rounded-lg">
        {/* Custom header */}
        <div className="bg-gray-100 p-2 border-b">
          <h2>My Spreadsheet</h2>
          {activeCell && <span>Selected: {activeCell.columnId}{activeCell.rowIndex + 1}</span>}
        </div>

        {/* Toolbar */}
        <Toolbar columns={columns} className="border-b" />

        {/* Formula bar */}
        <FormulaBar className="border-b" />

        {/* Main grid */}
        <SpreadsheetGrid className="flex-1" />

        {/* Custom footer */}
        <div className="bg-gray-50 p-2 border-t text-sm text-gray-600">
          Custom footer - <StatusBar totalRows={data.length} totalColumns={columns.length} selectedRowCount={0} visibleColumnIds={columns.map(c => c.id)} />
        </div>
      </div>
    </SpreadsheetProvider>
  );
}
```

#### Using Hooks Directly

Build your own components using hooks:

```tsx
import { useSpreadsheetStore, useHyperFormula } from "@shadsheet/ui";

function CustomFormulaBar() {
  const activeCell = useSpreadsheetStore((s) => s.activeCell);
  const hf = useHyperFormula();

  if (!activeCell || !hf) return null;

  const rawValue = hf.getCellFormula({ sheet: 0, row: activeCell.rowIndex, col: 0 });

  return (
    <input
      value={rawValue ?? ""}
      onChange={(e) => {
        // Update HyperFormula...
      }}
    />
  );
}
```

#### Standalone Store

Create a store outside React for testing or server-side logic:

```tsx
import { createSpreadsheetStore } from "@shadsheet/ui";

const store = createSpreadsheetStore(columns);

// Subscribe to changes
store.subscribe((state) => {
  console.log("Active cell:", state.activeCell);
});

// Dispatch actions
store.getState().setActiveCell({ rowIndex: 0, columnId: "A" });
```

### Custom Layouts

Guide on creating custom spreadsheet layouts with different components positioned as needed.

### Theming

Learn how to customize the appearance of ShadSheet components with Tailwind CSS and custom CSS variables.

### TypeScript

Detailed guide on using ShadSheet with TypeScript, including type definitions and generic components.
